one_liner: DE down 2.8% on 1.55x distribution volume at 90-day resistance ($618.96) — RBC says 2026 marks the cycle bottom, RSI 67 near overbought, DCF base case $199 (68% downside); priced for peak not trough.

price_action.intraday_story: DE opened at $622.77, touched $630.05 (above 90-day resistance at $618.96), then sold off to close at $613.24. A classic rejection at resistance: the stock tested above $618.96 and failed to hold it, closing below resistance. The 1.55x volume on the rejection is a bearish distribution signal.

news_catalyst.headline: RBC Capital Says 2026 Marks the Bottom of Agriculture Cycle — Raises Price Target on Deere After Strong Q2
news_catalyst.summary: RBC upgraded their view on DE, saying Q2 earnings suggest the agricultural downcycle is bottoming in 2026. This is a cycle bottom call — which theoretically supports buying now ahead of the recovery. However, the DCF shows $199 intrinsic value against $613 price, meaning the stock is pricing in an exceptional cycle recovery that hasn't happened yet. The rejection at resistance today (high of $630.05 vs resistance at $618.96) on elevated volume is the market's verdict on the RBC upgrade.

technical.summary: RSI at 67.18 is approaching overbought. The rejection at resistance ($618.96) on elevated distribution volume (1.55x) is the key technical signal. MACD bullish crossover is the only positive — but it's a lagging indicator. Resistance rejection at elevated RSI is typically a bearish setup.

wyckoff_narrative: No formal Wyckoff events. The resistance rejection pattern is consistent with an Upthrust (UT) in a distribution framework — but without prior BC/AR tags, it's more likely just a technical resistance test that failed. The 68% DCF downside is the most important context: even if RBC is right about a cycle bottom, the stock is priced for an extraordinary recovery that justifies current levels only in the most optimistic scenario.

valuation.summary: 68% downside to DCF base case. Even the bull case of $454 is 26% below current price. The stock is pricing in normalized agricultural earnings that require both a cycle recovery AND sustained growth — the conjunction of both is priced in at $613.

key_strengths: Precision agriculture technology provides long-term moat
key_concerns: Negative revenue growth — cycle trough confirmed
fundamentals.summary: Grade D reflects peak-cycle pricing on a cyclical trough earnings base. RBC's cycle bottom call may be right but the stock needs to reprice to reflect normalized rather than peak earnings.

risks: Rejection at $618.96 resistance on 1.55x volume — distribution day at the top of the range
RSI 67 near overbought — limited upside before a technical correction
68% downside to DCF base case — most overvalued name in watchlist on an absolute basis
